title: NOC 62010 – Retail Sales Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: Retail sales supervisors (NOC 62010) are the crucial link between business strategy and daily customer experience. This TEER 2 role is perfect for skilled professionals who excel in coordination and team leadership. In the modern Canadian economy, retail supervisors also play a key role in e-commerce fulfillment, making this a future-proof occupation for immigration. At GTR Immigration, we help supervisors utilize their experience to qualify for PR through CEC and targeted PNP draws.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6201 – Retail sales supervisorsNOC Code62010

duties: Supervise and coordinate the activities of sales staff and cashiers.
Assign duties and prepare work schedules for the team.
Resolve customer requests, complaints, and product shortages.
Maintain inventory and order merchandise from suppliers.
Hire, train, and monitor the performance of new sales staff.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Previous retail sales experience as a salesperson, cashier, or telemarketer is mandatory.
Specialized product knowledge may be required for certain departments (e.g., electronics or pharmaceuticals).
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. For the 2022-2031 period, new job openings are expected to total over 78,000 due to both industry growth and the retirement of existing workers.
labour_market_demand: The demand for retail supervisors is exceptionally strong across Canada due to the sheer size of the retail trade sector. With the shift toward omnichannel shopping, supervisors who can manage both in-store teams and online order fulfillment (JobMaze integration) are highly prized by Canadian employers.
Entry to Management: A clear stepping stone to Retail Trade Manager (NOC 60020) roles.
Job Availability: Retail is one of Canada’s largest employers with vacancies in every province.
Skill Diversity: Develop expertise in inventory management, marketing, and human resources.
pathways: Canadian Experience Class (CEC): The primary route for those with one year of Canadian work experience.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): Competitive for those with high language scores.
Saskatchewan Express Entry: Frequently includes retail supervisors in occupation-in-demand lists.
